stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of the Meta-Llama-3-8B model on Hugging Face.
It is categorized under key NLP/ML tags including 'transformers', 'llama', and 'text-generation'.
This indicates active community interest and benchmarking activity around a major LLM release.

stories.title: All of the updates from Elon Musk and Sam Altman’s battle over OpenAI
stories.author: Hayden Field
bullet_points: Elon Musk has sued OpenAI, alleging the company has deviated from its founding mission.
The core of the lawsuit claims OpenAI is prioritizing profit over its original goal of benefiting humanity.
The high-stakes legal battle could significantly impact the future direction of OpenAI and ChatGPT.

stories.title: Researcher says Microsoft secretly built a backdoor into BitLocker
stories.author: techspot.com via oceanhaiyang
bullet_points: Allegation of a secret backdoor built into Microsoft's BitLocker encryption.
The claim originates from a researcher, suggesting a high-stakes security vulnerability.
This news directly impacts the security and trust in major enterprise encryption software.

stories.title: CISA Admin Leaked AWS GovCloud Keys on Github
stories.author: krebsonsecurity.com via kngl
bullet_points: A CISA administrator allegedly leaked AWS GovCloud keys on GitHub.
This represents a critical cybersecurity incident involving sensitive government cloud infrastructure.
The leak poses a severe security risk to government data hosted on AWS GovCloud.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights a trending model, deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1, on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The model is categorized under text-generation and conversational AI pipelines.
It utilizes key ML technologies such as transformers and safetensors.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The content highlights the trending status of the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face.
It is categorized under text-generation, indicating its primary function as a large language model.
The tags confirm its relevance within the transformer and LLM ecosystem.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the 'bigscience/bloom' model trending on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The associated pipeline is focused on text-generation tasks.
Key technologies involved include transformers, PyTorch, and safetensors.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, is integrating MLX for performance optimization.
This integration specifically targets Apple Silicon, enhancing local AI capabilities on Mac devices.
The feature is currently available in a preview state, indicating ongoing development and optimization.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command for running coding tools locally or in the cloud.
It supports various models, including Claude Code, OpenCode, and Codex.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or config files.

stories.title: OpenAI gpt-oss-safeguard
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama is integrating OpenAI's gpt-oss-safeguard reasoning models for safety classification.
The models are available in two sizes (20B and 120B) and are released under the permissive Apache 2.0 license.
This partnership with OpenAI and ROOST aims to bring advanced safety tooling to the open-source community.

stories.title: PlayStation exclusives aren’t coming to PC anymore
stories.author: Jay Peters
bullet_points: Sony is reportedly ending the strategy of releasing major single-player PlayStation exclusives on PC.
The shift in strategy was communicated internally by Hermen Hulst, head of PlayStation's studios business.
This follows previous reports indicating Sony had already scrapped plans for PC launches.

stories.title: Code as Agent Harness
stories.author: Jingrui He
bullet_points: LLMs are evolving beyond just code generation to using code as an operational substrate for agent reasoning and action.
The concept of 'code as agent harness' unifies how code functions within emerging agentic systems.
This shift impacts software engineering by integrating code into agent modeling, execution, and verification processes.

stories.title: ESI-Bench: Towards Embodied Spatial Intelligence that Closes the Perception-Action Loop
stories.author: Yejin Choi
bullet_points: Introduces ESI-Bench, a new benchmark focused on embodied spatial intelligence.
Proposes moving beyond passive sensing by framing the observer as an active actor in a perception-action loop.
Aims to enable agents to uncover unseen information, such as occluded structure and dynamics, which passive sensing cannot resolve.

stories.title: Predictable Confabulations: Factual Recall by LLMs Scales with Model Size and Topic Frequency
stories.author: Tegawendé F. Bissyandé
bullet_points: The research establishes a novel scaling law linking factual recall in LLMs to both model size and the frequency of topics in the training data.
Factual recall quality was found to follow a sigmoid curve based on the log-linear combination of parameter count and topic representation.
The study analyzed 38 models against over 8,900 scholarly references, showing these two factors explain 60% of the variance in recall quality.

stories.title: ESI-Bench: Towards Embodied Spatial Intelligence that Closes the Perception-Action Loop
stories.author: Yejin Choi
bullet_points: Introduces ESI-Bench, a new benchmark focusing on Embodied Spatial Intelligence.
Proposes moving beyond passive sensing by framing the observer as an active actor in a perception-action loop.
Aims to allow agents to actively uncover unseen information, such as occluded structure and dynamics.

stories.title: Predictable Confabulations: Factual Recall by LLMs Scales with Model Size and Topic Frequency
stories.author: Tegawendé F. Bissyandé
bullet_points: The research establishes a new scaling law linking factual recall in LLMs to both model size and topic frequency in training data.
Factual recall quality exhibits a sigmoid relationship when combining model parameters and topic representation.
The study analyzed 38 models across 8,900 scholarly references, demonstrating a measurable link between training data composition and factual accuracy.

stories.title: EnvFactory: Scaling Tool-Use Agents via Executable Environments Synthesis and Robust RL
stories.author: Zhijiang Guo
bullet_points: Addresses key bottlenecks in scaling LLM tool-use agents: lack of robust execution environments and scarcity of realistic training data.
Proposes 'EnvFactory' to synthesize scalable and robust executable environments for Agentic Reinforcement Learning (Agentic RL).
Overcomes limitations of existing methods, such as reliance on costly real-world APIs or hallucination-prone simulators.

stories.title: Code as Agent Harness
stories.author: Jingrui He
bullet_points: Code is evolving from just a generated output of LLMs to an active operational substrate in agentic systems.
The paper introduces the concept of 'code as agent harness' to unify this functional shift.
This framework relates to how LLMs are used for reasoning, action, environment modeling, and verification in advanced agents.

stories.title: Predictable Confabulations: Factual Recall by LLMs Scales with Model Size and Topic Frequency
stories.author: Tegawendé F. Bissyandé
bullet_points: The research establishes a novel scaling law linking factual recall in LLMs to both model size and the frequency of topics in the training data.
Factual recall quality was found to follow a sigmoid curve based on the log-linear combination of model parameters and topic representation.
The study analyzed 38 models across over 8,900 scholarly references, demonstrating that these two factors explain 60% of the variance in dense models.

stories.title: Generative AI Advertising as a Problem of Trustworthy Commercial Intervention
stories.author: Qiaozhu Mei
bullet_points: Generative AI is fundamentally changing advertising by enabling interventions within the LLM generation process, not just in discrete slots.
Current deployed systems maintain a visible boundary between commercial content and AI responses, but this is often bypassed in practice.
The core problem identified is the lack of user detection when ads are subtly woven into LLM outputs, posing a trust issue.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging applications.
It utilizes local AI coding agents that run entirely on the user's device.
The core value proposition is providing AI assistance while maintaining data privacy through local execution.

stories.title: Claude Code with Anthropic API compatibility
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now supports compatibility with the Anthropic Messages API.
This integration allows users to leverage models like Claude Code within the Ollama ecosystem.
The feature bridges the gap between open-source local models and proprietary API capabilities.

stories.title: OpenAI Codex with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama enables the use of OpenAI's Codex CLI with open-source models.
Codex, when integrated, can read, modify, and execute code within a user's directory.
The system supports various open-weight alternatives, such as gpt-oss:20b and gpt-oss:120b.

stories.title: LG will release the first 1000Hz, 1080p gaming monitor this year
stories.author: Richard Lawler
bullet_points: LG is launching the UltraGear 25G590B gaming monitor.
This monitor features a native 1000Hz refresh rate at 1080p resolution.
The announcement addresses the trade-off between high refresh rates and resolution in gaming displays.

stories.title: Volvo teases a new affordable EV to replace discontinued EX30
stories.author: Andrew J. Hawkins
bullet_points: Volvo is planning a new, more affordable electric vehicle to replace the discontinued EX30.
The EX30 faced issues upon release, including affordability problems due to tariffs and a battery recall.
This move indicates Volvo's continued focus on expanding its portfolio of budget-friendly electric models.

stories.title: Elon Musk loses his case against Sam Altman
stories.author: Hayden Field
bullet_points: Elon Musk's lawsuit against Sam Altman reached a unanimous verdict.
The jury found that two claims in the case were barred by the statute of limitations.
A third claim failed due to the dismissal of one of the initial claims.

stories.title: Elon Musk took too long to sue OpenAI, jury unanimously agrees
stories.author: Ashley Belanger
bullet_points: Elon Musk sued OpenAI, and a jury unanimously sided against him.
The judge immediately affirmed the jury's verdict, preventing a quick reversal.
Musk intends to appeal the ruling following the jury's decision.

stories.title: DashAttention: Differentiable and Adaptive Sparse Hierarchical Attention
stories.author: Marcos V. Treviso
bullet_points: Introduces DashAttention, a new model for sparse hierarchical attention.
Addresses limitations in existing methods (like NSA/InfLLMv2) where top-k selection prevents gradient flow.
Proposes a differentiable and adaptive mechanism to improve the transition between sparse and dense attention stages.

stories.title: Vision-OPD: Learning to See Fine Details for Multimodal LLMs via On-Policy Self-Distillation
stories.author: Yaojie Lu
bullet_points: Multimodal LLMs (MLLMs) exhibit a 'regional-to-global perception gap' when handling fine-grained visual details.
The model's performance improves when conditioned on evidence-centered image crops rather than the full image, indicating a focus issue.
The research proposes 'Vision-OPD' to address this gap through on-policy self-distillation, aiming to improve evidence-based reasoning in MLLMs.

stories.title: What Does the AI Doctor Value? Auditing Pluralism in the Clinical Ethics of Language Models
stories.author: Gabriel A. Brat
bullet_points: The paper addresses the inherent pluralism and ethical conflicts in medical decision-making.
It proposes a framework to audit the value pluralism embedded within Large Language Models (LLMs) used in clinical settings.
The research highlights the need to examine how LLMs handle complex ethical dilemmas that divide physicians.

stories.title: DexHoldem: Playing Texas Hold'em with Dexterous Embodied System
stories.author: Yi Ma
bullet_points: Introduces DexHoldem, a real-world system-level benchmark for evaluating embodied AI systems.
The benchmark uses Texas Hold'em as a task to test perception, context-appropriate action selection, and dexterous manipulation.
It leverages a ShadowHand and includes 1,470 teleoperated demonstrations across 14 manipulation primitives.

stories.title: Semantic Generative Tuning for Unified Multimodal Models
stories.author: Yanwei Li
bullet_points: The research addresses the challenge in Unified Multimodal Models (UMMs) where understanding and generation are trained independently.
Current decoupled training leads to misaligned representation spaces, preventing mutual reinforcement between visual understanding and generation.
The paper introduces a systematic investigation into 'Semantic Generative Tuning' to unify these processes within a single architecture.

stories.title: Distilling Tabular Foundation Models for Structured Health Data
stories.author: Pratinav Seth
bullet_points: The research addresses the high inference cost of Tabular Foundation Models (TFMs) used in health data.
It proposes using knowledge distillation to transfer the predictive power of large TFMs to lightweight models.
A novel technique, stratified out-of-fold teacher labeling, is used to prevent context leakage during distillation.

stories.title: DashAttention: Differentiable and Adaptive Sparse Hierarchical Attention
stories.author: Marcos V. Treviso
bullet_points: Proposes DashAttention, a new method for hierarchical attention in ML models.
Addresses limitations in existing methods (like NSA/InfLLMv2) where top-k selection prevents gradient flow.
Introduces a differentiable and adaptive approach to sparse hierarchical attention.

stories.title: Vision-OPD: Learning to See Fine Details for Multimodal LLMs via On-Policy Self-Distillation
stories.author: Yaojie Lu
bullet_points: Addresses the current limitation of Multimodal LLMs (MLLMs) in achieving fine-grained visual understanding.
Identifies a 'regional-to-global perception gap,' where MLLMs perform better with evidence-centered crops than full images.
Proposes 'Vision-OPD,' a method using On-Policy Self-Distillation to help models focus on relevant visual evidence.

stories.title: PIXLRelight: Controllable Relighting via Intrinsic Conditioning
stories.author: Ronald Clark
bullet_points: PIXLRelight introduces a feed-forward method for physically controllable single-image relighting.
It addresses limitations in existing methods, such as poor lighting control or error accumulation in rendering chains.
The core innovation is bridging Physically Based Rendering (PBR) and learned synthesis using shared intrinsic conditioning.

stories.title: General Preference Reinforcement Learning
stories.author: John M. Cioffi
bullet_points: Current LLM alignment is split: Online RL excels at verifiable tasks (math/code) but lacks open-ended capability.
Preference optimization handles open-ended generation but lacks the continuous exploration found in online RL.
The research identifies the need for a verifier capable of assessing open-ended quality to bridge the gap between these two alignment methods.

stories.title: DashAttention: Differentiable and Adaptive Sparse Hierarchical Attention
stories.author: Marcos V. Treviso
bullet_points: Introduces DashAttention, a novel hierarchical attention mechanism designed to improve upon existing methods like NSA and InfLLMv2.
Addresses a key limitation in current sparse attention methods: the fixed top-k selection process prevents proper gradient flow between sparse and dense attention stages.
The proposed method is both 'Differentiable' and 'Adaptive,' allowing for more flexible and trainable selection of relevant key-value blocks.

stories.title: ESI-Bench: Towards Embodied Spatial Intelligence that Closes the Perception-Action Loop
stories.author: Yejin Choi
bullet_points: Introduces ESI-Bench, a new benchmark for testing embodied spatial intelligence.
Focuses on closing the perception-action loop, where agents actively interact with the environment to gather information.
Moves beyond passive sensing by enabling agents to uncover unseen details like occluded structure and dynamics.

stories.title: Vision-OPD: Learning to See Fine Details for Multimodal LLMs via On-Policy Self-Distillation
stories.author: Yaojie Lu
bullet_points: Multimodal LLMs (MLLMs) exhibit a 'regional-to-global perception gap,' struggling with fine-grained visual understanding.
The model performs better on evidence-centered image crops than on the full image for detailed questions.
The research proposes 'Vision-OPD' to address this by learning to focus on relevant visual evidence via on-policy self-distillation.

stories.title: General Preference Reinforcement Learning
stories.author: John M. Cioffi
bullet_points: Current LLM alignment is split between online RL (good for verifiable tasks like math/code) and preference optimization (good for open-ended generation).
Online RL is limited by the need for a programmatic verifier, which fails on open-ended tasks.
The core challenge identified is the need for a verifier capable of assessing open-ended quality, suggesting current scalar reward models are insufficient.

stories.title: EnvFactory: Scaling Tool-Use Agents via Executable Environments Synthesis and Robust RL
stories.author: Zhijiang Guo
bullet_points: The paper addresses limitations in scaling LLM tool-use agents, specifically the lack of robust and scalable execution environments.
It tackles the scarcity of realistic training data, moving beyond costly real-world APIs and hallucination-prone simulators.
The proposed method, EnvFactory, synthesizes executable environments to improve Agentic Reinforcement Learning (Agentic RL) training.

stories.title: GIM: Evaluating models via tasks that integrate multiple cognitive domains
stories.author: Steven McClain
bullet_points: The paper introduces GIM (Grounded Integration Measure), a new benchmark for evaluating Large Language Models (LLMs).
GIM addresses limitations in current benchmarks (like GPQA and ARC-AGI) by moving beyond simple knowledge recall or purely abstract reasoning.
The benchmark consists of 820 original problems designed to test models across multiple integrated cognitive domains.

stories.title: An Assessment of Human vs. Model Uncertainty in Soft-Label Learning and Calibration
stories.author: Massimo Poesio
bullet_points: The research investigates the role of human-elicited 'soft-labels' in improving AI model calibration.
It addresses a prior conflation in the literature, separating the benefits of human uncertainty capture from the correction of mislabeled data (mode shifts).
The study uses a controlled audit on MNIST and a synthetic variant to decouple and measure the true effect of soft-label supervision.

stories.title: Mini Shai-Hulud Strikes Again: 314 npm Packages Compromised
stories.author: theanonymousone
bullet_points: A significant security incident involving the compromise of 314 npm packages has been reported.
The incident is referenced with a dramatic title, suggesting a widespread or severe nature of the vulnerability.
The source is Hacker News, indicating the topic is relevant to the developer and open-source community.

stories.title: I red-teamed Oracle APEX 26.1's new AI Agent feature in the 72 hours after it went GA. Claude refused 7 of my 10 attacks on its own.
stories.author: rkondoju
bullet_points: The article details a red-teaming exercise performed on Oracle APEX 26.1's new AI Agent feature.
The testing involved mapping 10 attack patterns against Anthropic Claude Sonnet 4.6.
The analysis revealed specific attack classes that succeeded despite Claude's defenses and identified a bug in APEX 26.1.
